title: podman commands fail when run as non-root user with "Error: cannot re-exec process"
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  I'm not sure how to reproduce this issue, it has suddenly begun to show up on nodes that are part of a Jenkins CI

**Describe the results you received:**
```
$ podman ps -a
Error: cannot re-exec process
$
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
$
```



**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.2
API Version:  4.0.2
Go Version:   go1.17.7

Built:      Tue Mar 15 19:15:06 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-4.0.2-1.module_el8.7.0+1106+45480ee0.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**

The latest version available of podman is 4.0.2 on my system.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

seen on a physical machine running CentOS Stream 8 (the system is up-to-date)

```
$ sudo dnf update
Last metadata expiration check: 0:06:30 ago on Fri 17 Jun 2022 04:49:35 AM UTC.
Dependencies resolved.
Nothing to do.
Complete!
```
no containers are running and the system isn't running under a high load.

I thought it could be https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13737 but I don't see any `pause.pid` file in `/run/user` :

```
$ id -u
10102
$ find /run/user/10102/ -name "*.pid"
$ sudo find /run/user/ -name "*.pid"
$
```

so I'm opening a new issue for that. Let me know if I have to share any other detail.

comments.body: can you strace the process with `strace -f podman ps -a`
do you have any file under `/run/containers/storage` (as it seems to be the runroot path from the info you posted above)?

```
# ls -l /run/containers/storage/*
/run/containers/storage/overlay:
total 4
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root   0 May  9 08:27 'metacopy(nodev,metacopy=on)-true'
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 141 May  9 08:27 'native-diff(nodev,metacopy=on)-false'
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root   0 May  9 08:27  overlay-true

/run/containers/storage/overlay-containers:
total 0

/run/containers/storage/overlay-layers:
total 0
-rw-r--r--. 1 root root 0 May  9 08:27 mountpoints.lock

/run/containers/storage/overlay-locks:
total 0
```

> can you strace the process with `strace -f podman ps -a`

[strace_podman_ps_-a.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/8927210/strace_podman_ps_-a.log)
I've opened a PR that could help to understand what is going on here: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/14638

the problem with strace is that `newuidmap/newgidmap` will fail since they are traced, so the error we are seeing is probably caused by that.

Could you try to strace the parent process (the shell where you run the podman command in) as root (`sudo strace -v -s 10000 -f -p $PARENT_PID`)?

> the problem with strace is that `newuidmap/newgidmap` will fail since they are traced, so the error we are seeing is probably caused by that.
> 
> Could you try to strace the parent process (the shell where you run the podman command in) as root (`sudo strace -v -s 10000 -f -p $PARENT_PID`)?

[strace_podman_ps_-a_take2.log](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/8928561/strace_podman_ps_-a_take2.log)

[https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9137#issuecomment-771859402](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/9137#issuecomment-771859402)
This worked for me but the path of `pause.pid` is in `/tmp/podman-run-1000/libpod/tmp/pause.pid`. Maybe you can find for `pause.pid` in `tmp`.

sorry i didn't update that issue before but yes, it turns out `pause.pid` was in `/tmp` too in my case.
@giuseppe suspects this is because the machine running podman was probably provisioned with a 'golden image'.

Deleting the pid-file is also a workaround for us. But this is only temporary and it sporadically happens again. We do have multiple build agents running a lot of jobs (also Jenkins), which is why it happens more often. Might have to open an official business-case bug-ticket with RedHat soon if nobody has an idea how to permanently fix this...

> Deleting the pid-file is also a workaround for us. But this is only temporary and it sporadically happens again. We do have multiple build agents running a lot of jobs (also Jenkins), which is why it happens more often. 

indeed, the situation I'm facing is quite similar. I had to do something like this [1] to work around this issue.

[1] https://github.com/ceph/ceph-container/commit/a0d1e714aaa813db6cdf15a4a6ea87ba9525d803

This is kind of the same workaround we do use. But still, this is only a workaround and podman sporadically breaks our build pipelines stopping the devs from being able to work thus costing money until the workaround script was started. We do need a permanent fix for this.

please provide the output of `podman info` from the same environment where the issue appears.

In the original issue, I see the `podman info` output when it is executed as root, but the error is clearly happening as rootless in a different environment.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we never heard back, I am closing this issue,  Reopen if you have feedback for @giuseppe
